goal: Prove the dispatch-readiness data flow composes end-to-end in DRY-RUN by wiring the existing chain workerPacketWlpAdapter -> cardConditionedDispatch -> preRegisteredClaims -> dispatchFrontendReadiness -> runtimeDispatchIntegrationContract into one read-only composition module exercised by an executed test that turns a sample worker packet into a dispatch-readiness result. No provider, write, route, DB or orchestrator change. After this task the chain modules are non_live_value_referenced (a real used value importer exists, but it is not yet live-reachable); live exposure is WIRE-SLICE-002. Also backfill a retroactive WLP record for cardConditionedDispatch. Done means the chain runs through in dry-run, proven by test.
scope_out: Do not call any provider, write any file, mount any route, touch the DB or modify the orchestrator. Do not drag in the cockpit/memory/release lattice beyond reading the named runtime dispatch modules. The slice is a pure dry-run composition exercised only by its test; it is NOT yet live-reachable, and the contract must not claim it is.
claims: workerPacketWlpAdapter can turn a sample worker packet into the input planCardConditionedDispatch(input) expects.

The chain adapter -> cardConditionedDispatch -> preRegisteredClaims -> dispatchFrontendReadiness -> runtimeDispatchIntegrationContract can be composed as a side-effect-free dry-run yielding a dispatch-readiness result.

After this task the chain modules become non_live_value_referenced: they have a real used value importer (dispatchDryRunSlice) but serverReachable stays false (not yet reachable from builder/src/server.ts).
No orchestrator, route, provider, write or DB change is required for the dry-run proof.
assumptions: All five chain modules exist and expose pure functions over explicit inputs.
A dry-run readiness result is sufficient end-to-end proof; live exposure is WIRE-SLICE-002.
WIRE-GATE-001 is live, so this contract's orphan_gate: enforce is honored; baseline_ref is e328ef0.
dod: builder/src/dispatchDryRunSlice.ts value-imports and actually uses all five chain modules, exposing one dry-run entry that composes them into a dispatch-readiness result with no side effects.

Because the slice is not yet live-reachable, dispatchDryRunSlice.ts carries '// @orphan-by-design: dry-run dispatch composition; live exposure in WIRE-SLICE-002; consumer = future route/orchestrator'.
builder/tests/wireSlice001DispatchDryRun.test.ts executes the composition on a sample worker packet and asserts each chain step is actually invoked and a readiness result is produced.

node tools/orphan-scan.cjs shows cardConditionedDispatch, workerPacketWlpAdapter and preRegisteredClaims now list dispatchDryRunSlice as a used value importer, with summary state non_live_value_referenced (serverReachable still false).
review-packets/cardConditionedDispatch-backfill.md is a retroactive WLP record: purpose, input/output types, prior build task, and the new used value importer with file path.
Focused test, builder typecheck, orphan-scan, task-lock verify and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Composing the chain requires a real provider call, a real write, a route mount, a DB call or an orchestrator change.
The slice cannot stay a side-effect-free dry-run.
More than the named composition module, its test and the docs must change.
prior_task_findings: WIRE-CENSUS-001 measured the three differentiators as not runtime_value_connected despite type-only references. WIRE-GATE-001 now enforces visible orphan exceptions for new builder/src modules. After this slice the modules are non_live_value_referenced, NOT live-connected.
